reasoning_model_1: The model fails the primary criterion of Content Accuracy. The user requested the specific text 'Get out of the way, don't block my path!', but the model generated 'Get out of the way, do you dare stand in my path, seriously'. This is a hallucination where the second half of the sentence is completely rewritten and an extra word ('seriously') is added at the end. While the audio does convey a very rude and aggressive tone (shouting), the severity of the content deviation—changing the words entirely—renders the generation 'clearly inconsistent with the script'. According to the scoring standard, significant content errors where the voice content is inconsistent with the script result in a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: The model demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy, reading the script 'Get out of the way, don't block my path!' exactly as requested [00:00-00:03]. The Fluency and Naturalness are high, with no mechanical artifacts or stuttering. regarding the Paralinguistic Compliance (Attitude), the model successfully adopts a rude, annoyed, and dismissive tone. The emphasis on 'Get out' and the lower, firmer pitch on 'don't block my path' effectively convey the requested hostile attitude without being overly theatrical. It is a natural and accurate representation of the prompt.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 suffers from a critical failure in Content Accuracy, hallucinating a different sentence ('do you dare stand in my path, seriously') instead of the requested text. Although Model 1's tone was indeed rude, the failure to follow the script invalidates the generation based on the strict evaluation criteria for content consistency. Model 2 followed the text perfectly and delivered a high-quality, natural audio performance with the correct rude attitude.
